steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the Columbia Trail and the Flint Hills Trail. The notes tell us both are rail trails — that is the shared trait. The correct answer must connect both trails to that common feature.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Columbia Trail is in New Jersey, whereas the Flint Hills Trail is in Kansas.': Highlights a difference (different states), not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice B — 'A former railroad corridor, the Columbia Trail is located in New Jersey.': Mentions only one trail. No comparison to the Flint Hills Trail. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Both the Columbia Trail and the Flint Hills Trail are rail trails.': Directly names both trails and states the trait they share. Exactly what the question asks for. ✓
  • Choice D — 'A public path created from a former railroad corridor can be found in Kansas.': Describes a trail in Kansas without naming the Flint Hills Trail, and does not mention the Columbia Trail at all. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
